title: Interaction between ibc and stmvp
abstract: A visual information decoding method includes determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique, determining that a spatially neighboring block of the block being decoded is coded using a second coding technique, deciding that the spatially neighboring block cannot provide the motion information to derive the motion vectors for the block being decoded; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and by excluding use of spatially neighboring block with a second decoding technique corresponding to the second coding technique. One of the first and second coding techniques corresponds to a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction coding (STMVP) technique and the other corresponds to an intra-block copy (IBC) technique that uses a second block of a same video picture for coding the block being decoded.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique; determining that a spatially neighboring block of the block being decoded is coded using a second coding technique; deciding that the spatially neighboring block cannot provide the motion information to derive the motion vectors for the block being decoded; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and by excluding use of spatially neighboring block with a second decoding technique corresponding to the second coding technique; wherein one of the first and second coding techniques corresponds to a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction coding (STMVP) technique and the other corresponds to an intra-block copy (IBC) technique that uses a second block of a same video picture for coding the block being decoded. 2. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique; determining that a spatially neighboring block of the block being decoded is coded using a second coding technique; deciding that the spatially neighboring block can provide motion information to derive motion vectors for the block being decoded but differently than providing the motion information by a spatially neighboring block coded not using the second coding technique; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and by using the motion information of spatially neighboring block conditionally based on whether the spatially neighboring block is coded with a second decoding technique corresponding to the second coding technique; 

 wherein one of the first and second coding techniques corresponds to a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction coding (STMVP) technique and the other corresponds to an intra-block copy (IBC) technique that uses a second block of a same video picture for coding the block being decoded. 3. The method of claims 1 or 2, further comprising decoding a first encoded block using the IBC decoding technique by: obtaining a first plurality of encoded blocks representing the first encoded picture; decoding an initial block in the first plurality of encoded blocks; and upon decoding the initial block, decoding a first block in the first plurality of encoded blocks based on the initial block. 4. The method of claims 1-3, comprising decoding a encoded block using the STMVP decoding technique by: obtaining a first plurality of encoded blocks representing the encoded picture including an encoded block being decoded; obtaining a first plurality of encoded sub-blocks representing the encoded block being decoded; obtaining a plurality of spatial neighbors of an encoded sub-block in the first plurality of encoded sub-blocks, and motion vectors of the encoded sub-block; and decoding the encoded sub-block based on the motion vectors and the plurality of spatial neighbors. 5. The method of claims 1-4, comprising: obtaining an indicator associated with a spatial neighbor of the encoded sub-block, the indicator signaling that the spatial neighbor is intra-coded, or that the spatial neighbor is unavailable for use in STMVP; and avoiding the use of the spatial neighbor in decoding the encoded sub-block. 6. The method of claims 1-5, comprising: 

 obtaining the indicator associated with the spatial neighbor of the encoded sub-block, the indicator signaling the spatial neighbor is intra-coded, or that the spatial neighbor is unavailable for use in STMVP; obtaining a motion information of the encoded sub-block; and decoding the encoded sub-block based on the motion information and the indicator. 7. The method of claim 6, comprising: determining that more than one spatial neighbor in the plurality of spatial neighbors is encoded with the IBC technique; obtaining a motion vector of the encoded sub-block; and decoding the encoded sub-block based on the motion vector and the more than one spatial neighbor encoded with the IBC decoding technique. 8. The method of claims 1-7, comprising: obtaining a plurality of STMVP candidates wherein at least one STMVP candidate in the plurality of STMVP candidates is derived from spatially neighboring pictures using non-current pictures of all sub-blocks, and at least one STMVP candidate in the plurality of STMVP candidates is derived from spatially neighboring pictures using current pictures of all sub-blocks. 9. A method for encoding a visual information, comprising: encoding the visual information into a plurality of encoded pictures and a plurality of indicators signaling one or more encoding techniques applied, the plurality of indicators comprising an intra-coded indicator, an unavailable indicator, and a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction (STMVP) technique indicator, wherein a first block of a first picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the IBC technique and a second block of a second picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the STMVP technique, wherein the IBC technique uses a block of the first picture to encode a different block of the first picture and the STMVP technique uses a third picture associated with the visual information to encode the second picture. 10. The method of claim 9, comprising encoding a first block using the IBC technique in the first picture by: dividing the first picture into a first plurality of blocks; encoding an initial block in the first plurality of blocks; and upon encoding the initial block, encoding a first block in the first plurality of blocks based on the initial block. 11. The method of claims 9-10, comprising encoding a second block using the STM VP technique in the second picture by: dividing the second picture into a second plurality of blocks including a block being encoded; dividing the block being encoded into a plurality of sub-blocks; identifying a plurality of spatial neighbors of a sub-block in the plurality of sub-blocks, wherein a plurality of indicators associated with the plurality of spatial neighbors indicate that the plurality of spatial neighbors is available for use in the STMVP technique, and wherein the plurality of indicators may be signaled or inferred; obtaining the motion information associated with the plurality of spatial neighbors; and encoding motion vectors of the sub-block based on the motion information associated with the plurality of spatial neighbors. 12. The method of claim 11, said identifying plurality of spatial neighbors comprising: obtaining a first neighboring block neighboring the sub-block in an upward direction and a first indicator indicating whether the first neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique; and when the first indicator signals that the first neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique, identifying the first neighboring block as one of the plurality of spatial neighbors. 13. The method of claims 11-12, said identifying plurality of spatial neighbors comprising: 

 when the first indicator indicates that the first neighbouring block is not available for use in the STMVP technique, searching for another neighbouring block in a direction left of the first sub-block until another indicator associated with the another neighboring block indicates that the another neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique. 14. The method of clai s 11-13, said identifying plurality of spatial neighbors comprising: obtaining a second neighboring block neighboring the neighboring block in a leftward direction and a second indicator indicating whether the second neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique; and when the second indicator indicates that the second neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique, identifying the second neighboring block as one of the plurality of spatial neighbors. 15. The method of claims 11-14, said identifying plurality of spatial neighbors comprising: when the second indicator indicates that the second neighboring block is not available for use in the STMVP technique, searching for another neighboring block in a direction downward of the second neighboring block until another indicator associated with the another neighboring block indicates that the another neighboring block is available for use in the STMVP technique. 16. The method of claims 9-15, comprising: encoding an indicator associated with a spatial neighbor of the sub-block to signal that the spatial neighbor is unavailable for use in STMVP when the spatial neighbor is encoded using the IBC technique. 17. The method of claims 9-15, comprising: encoding a spatial neighbor of the sub-block using the IBC technique; copying a motion information of the spatial neighbor to the motion information of the sub-block without scaling the motion information of the spatial neighbor; and encoding the sub-block using the IBC technique. 18. The method of clai s 6 or 17, comprising: avoiding to access the motion information of a co-located temporal neighboring block if the sub-block is encoded using the IBC technique. 19. The method of claim 17, comprising: determining that more than one spatial neighbor in the plurality of spatial neighbors is encoded with the IBC technique; obtaining more than one sets of motion information associated with the more than one spatial neighbor; and encoding the motion vectors of the sub-block based on the more than one sets of motion information. 20. The method of claims 9 to 19, comprising: creating a plurality of STMVP candidates wherein at least one STMVP candidate in the plurality of STMVP candidates is derived from spatially neighboring pictures using non-current pictures of all sub-blocks, and at least one STMVP candidate in the plurality of STMVP candidates is derived from spatially neighboring pictures using current pictures of all sub-blocks. 21. A video processing apparatus comprising a processor configured to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 20. 22. A computer readable medium having processor-executable code stored thereon, the code, upon execution, causing a processor to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 20.
